2018-10-02.abstract: North Korea says it will escalate its demands in its negotiations with the U.S. if Washington insists on too much in return.

2018-10-07.abstract: Secretary of State Mike Pompeo also said that during his fourth trip to Pyongyang he had discussed a new meeting between President Trump and the North’s leader, Kim Jong-un.

2018-10-25.title: Why Jamal Khashoggi’s Killing Has Resonated
2018-10-25.abstract: The killing of a dissident writer has set off international outrage in a way that previous Saudi actions have not. Here's why his death has had such broad geopolitical implications.

2018-10-25.title: Denmark Plans a Fence to Stop a Pig Disease. Will It Work?
2018-10-25.abstract: The problem, African swine fever, is serious, but critics say Denmark’s answer is not, and infected wild boar will find their way in from Germany.

2018-10-25.title: Pakistan Tries a New Way to Pay for a Dam: Crowdsourcing
2018-10-25.abstract: The financially challenged government asks Pakistanis at home and abroad to donate to a $14 billion dam project. It’s a sign of a much bigger problem.

2018-10-25.title: China Mocks Report It Tapped Trump’s iPhone (and Plugs Its Own Competitor)
2018-10-25.abstract: China ridiculed but did not explicitly deny reports by intelligence officials that the Chinese tapped President Trump’s phone calls with old friends.

2018-10-26.title: China Kindergarten Stabbing Injures 14 Children
2018-10-26.abstract: Mass shootings are rare in China, which has strict gun control. But attackers with knives sometimes carry out mass stabbings, and children are often the targets.

2018-10-26.title: Japan’s Leader Goes to Beijing. Here’s What’s at Stake.
2018-10-26.abstract: After a bitter and bloody history dating back to World War II, Japan and China are mending ties, and there’s one big reason: Donald Trump.

2018-10-26.title: A Day on the Road With the Migrant Caravan
2018-10-26.abstract: The political frenzy is heightening over a so-called migrant caravan heading toward the United States, but who are the people making the grueling journey? We spent 24 hours with a family as they trekked through Mexico for an up-close view of what’s at stake.

2018-10-26.title: The Tragedy of Saudi Arabia’s War in Yemen
2018-10-26.abstract: The Khashoggi killing has cast light on Saudi tactics in Yemen, where an economic war has pushed millions to the brink of starvation.

2018-10-26.title: ‘Falling Stars Challenge’ Takes the Humble Out of Humblebragging
2018-10-26.abstract: The meme began as an unusual way to show off one’s wealth, but it’s taken on new meaning while spreading across Asia.

2018-10-27.title: A #MeToo Story in Britain Prompts Debate Over Privacy Laws
2018-10-27.abstract: The uproar followed a court’s decision to prevent a British newspaper from publishing accusations of misconduct against a well-known businessman.

2018-10-27.title: On Foot and Underground at Canada’s Crossroads
2018-10-27.abstract: Voters in Winnipeg overwhelmingly rejected plans to reopen Portage and Main to pedestrians after 40 years.

2018-10-27.title: Salvini Seizes on 2nd Italian Teenager’s Death to Push Immigration Issue
2018-10-27.abstract: The alleged rape of an overdosed 16-year-old girl by African migrants has given more leverage to populist leader, who not may have Rome in his sights.

2018-10-27.title: ‘They Don’t Have Money’: Greece’s Prostitutes Hit Hard by Financial Crisis
2018-10-27.abstract: An industry that has seen most everything is suffering under rock-bottom prices after a decade-long crisis left no profession unscathed, degrading the lives of Greek citizens.

2018-10-27.title: Saudi Arabia Rejects Turkey’s Extradition Request in Khashoggi Killing
2018-10-27.abstract: The Saudi foreign minister said the kingdom, not Turkey, would prosecute the suspects and called the backlash “hysterical.”

2018-10-28.title: Bishops Urge Greater Inclusion of Women in Church Decisions
2018-10-28.abstract: A Vatican document presented to the pope says that the church needs ideas and more women in decision-making roles.

2018-10-28.title: A Momentous Election in Brazil: What’s at Stake?
2018-10-28.abstract: Concerns about crime, corruption and morality have given a far-right lawmaker the upper hand in Brazil’s presidential election. Here’s a primer.

2018-10-28.title: Jair Bolsonaro’s Been Called a Misogynist and Fascist. Here’s Why Women Still Back Him.
2018-10-28.abstract: Jair Bolsonaro, Brazil’s newly elected president, is known for his offensive remarks about women, but his hard-line agenda on crime has spurred many to vote for him. We heard from women on both sides.

2018-10-28.title: Vichai Srivaddhanaprabha, Leicester City Owner, Is Dead After Chopper Crash
2018-10-28.abstract: The club announced that Vichai had died in the crash outside its stadium after a game on Saturday.

2018-10-28.title: As Rich Nations Close the Door on Refugees, Uganda Welcomes Them
2018-10-28.abstract: In Uganda, refugees are given land, the right to work, and freedom to move around. And far from prompting a backlash, Ugandans remain welcoming of refugees.

2018-10-29.title: Indonesia Plane Crash Adds to Country’s Troubling Safety Record
2018-10-29.abstract: A brand-new plane flying in clear skies crashed shortly after takeoff, and 189 people were feared dead.

2018-10-29.title: South Korea’s Leader Vouches for Kim’s Sincerity, as Critics See Deception
2018-10-29.abstract: The South’s president, Moon Jae-in, says Kim Jong-un truly wants to trade nuclear arms for prosperity. Critics say Mr. Moon is being duped by yet another deceitful dictator.

2018-10-29.title: Vancouver, Canada’s Marijuana Capital, Struggles to Tame the Black Market
2018-10-29.abstract: As Canada embarks on a national experiment to legalize marijuana, Vancouver illustrates the difficulties of policing the illegal trade.

2018-10-29.title: Brazil Election: How Jair Bolsonaro Turned Crisis Into Opportunity
2018-10-29.abstract: A troubled stint in the army and a polemic three-decade career as a lawmaker preceded Mr. Bolsonaro’s unlikely rise to the presidency.

2018-10-29.title: Germany Without Angela Merkel: Unthinkable? Think Again, She Says
2018-10-29.abstract: The German chancellor is stepping down as head of the Christian Democrats, setting up a power struggle within her party.

2018-10-30.title: How a World War II-Era Reparations Case Is Roiling Asia
2018-10-30.abstract: More than seven decades later, South Korea’s top court ruled that Japanese companies must pay for their use of Korean slave labor during World War II.

2018-10-30.title: Austerity Is Over, Britain Says, Despite Brexit Uncertainty
2018-10-30.abstract: The chancellor of the Exchequer promised more money for health, education and even fixing potholes. But worry remains about the country’s withdrawal from the European Union.

2018-10-30.title: Did a Small Metal Tube Bring Down an Indonesian Airliner?
2018-10-30.abstract: As teams search for Lion Air Flight 610’s black boxes, experts speculate that a faulty pitot tube, used for measuring airspeed, may have contributed to the crash.

2018-10-30.title: ‘They Threw Themselves Into the Sea, 14 Black Women, All Together’
2018-10-30.abstract: Nantes played a major role in the European slave trade. Its museum and memorial are a model for other French cities grappling with their histories.

2018-10-30.title: Chinese Military May Gain From Western University Ties, Report Warns
2018-10-30.abstract: A new report from Australia says the West is taking risks by collaborating with researchers tied to the Chinese military.

2018-10-31.title: Rescue Workers Search for Missing After Typhoon Strikes Philippines
2018-10-31.abstract: Nine bodies were recovered Wednesday from the site of a government building that was buried by a landslide after Typhoon Yutu struck the country’s north.

2018-10-31.title: India Unveils Statue of Unity, World’s Tallest Statue and Twice the Size of Lady Liberty
2018-10-31.abstract: The bronze Statue of Unity pays tribute to Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel, a leader of the Indian independence movement who helped wrest the country from British control.

2018-10-31.title: Pakistani Court Acquits Christian Woman in Capital Blasphemy Case
2018-10-31.abstract: Asia Bibi had been on death row for eight years, and her case helped focus international attention on Pakistan’s harsh blasphemy law.

2018-10-31.title: How the Far Right Is Shaking Germany’s Political Order
2018-10-31.abstract: Germany’s role as the defender of a liberal and tolerant Europe is in jeopardy: A far-right political party is gaining strength and Chancellor Angela Merkel says she will not seek re-election. We went to Chemnitz, where the far right feels emboldened and is protesting in the streets.

2018-10-31.title: Once at Guantánamo, 5 Senior Taliban Members Now Join Political Office in Qatar
2018-10-31.abstract: The insurgent group said the five leaders who were traded for Sgt. Bowe Bergdahl in 2014 have been shifting to active roles just as peace talks are being pushed.
